publication.title: The mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c promotes metabolic homeostasis and reduces obesity and insulin resistance

study_design: Discovery study combining molecular, cell and mouse experiments.
population_or_model: Cell systems and multiple mouse metabolic models; endogenous peptide detection included human samples.
intervention_or_exposure: Experimental MOTS-c exposure in cells and mice; mouse studies included intraperitoneal treatment.
outcomes: Identified a mitochondrial 12S-rRNA short open reading frame encoding a 16-amino-acid peptide.
Mechanistic data implicated folate/purine metabolism and AMPK activation.
Skeletal muscle was a principal target in the study.
Mouse treatment prevented age- and high-fat-diet-related insulin resistance and diet-induced obesity in the reported models.
The evidence was not a human therapeutic intervention trial.
limitations: Mouse injection data do not establish human dosing.
No human efficacy or long-term safety conclusion.
permitted_claims: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived 16-amino-acid peptide studied in metabolic cell and mouse models.
The study linked MOTS-c to AMPK-related metabolic signalling.
prohibited_claims: Claim that MOTS-c reverses human obesity or diabetes.
Translation of animal study dosing into human use.
source_closure: PRIMARY ABSTRACT AND FIGURE-LEVEL CORE METHODS DIRECTLY EXTRACTED
